findings.evidence: Current theorem is under `[FiniteDimensional ℝ V]` at lines 100-135; `IsLagrangian` is just `ω.orthogonal L = L` at `Lagrangian.lean:94`, and `Invariant` gives `ω (J v) (J w) = ω v w` at `AlmostComplex.lean:315`, which suffices without dimension counting.
findings.fix: Move `IsLagrangian.map_of_invariant` outside the finite-dimensional section and prove it from `IsLagrangian : ω.orthogonal L = L` using `J.linearEquiv`/`J ( -J x ) = x` and `ω.Invariant J`, rather than via `isLagrangian_of_finrank`.
findings.issue: `IsLagrangian.map_of_invariant` is inside `section FiniteDimensional`, but invariance of `ω` under the linear equivalence `J` preserves the symplectic orthogonal directly, so finite dimensionality is an unnecessary assumption.

summary: One theorem is stated at a needlessly finite-dimensional level; the Lagrangian image result is algebraic and should not depend on the half-dimension API.
